ping reviewers - need to review for potential inclusion in 2.10.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

the patch itself looks good to me, but it's more desirable for client to be able to try bits.

Patch Set 5:

Jinshan, a very interesting idea. Is there a reason why that couldn't be done after this patch is landed?

Patch Set 5:

> Jinshan, a very interesting idea. Is there a reason why that
 > couldn't be done after this patch is landed?

The code looks good to me so I am against landing it as-is. However, it wouldn't add much extra work by doing so based on the current state of the patch. Let's wait for Mike's input.

Patch Set 5:

> > Jinshan, a very interesting idea. Is there a reason why that
 > > couldn't be done after this patch is landed?
 > 
 > The code looks good to me so I am against landing it as-is.
 > However, it wouldn't add much extra work by doing so based on the
 > current state of the patch. Let's wait for Mike's input.

s/against/not against/

Patch Set 5:

I agree that this can be done, I didn't do that just because there is no use cases for trying bits on client now. If you have some idea about that, please add comment in the ticket. I am OK to continue with the new patch about client side trybits, but I'd land this one first.

parents.subject: LU-8066 ptlrpc: migrate ptlrpc proc files to sysfs

commit.subject: LU-9184 ldlm: selective IBITS lock trying
commit.message:
LU-9184 ldlm: selective IBITS lock trying

There is code to get lock without blocking, e.g. 'try'
to get it. It enqueues lock with all wanted bits and
get it only when all bits are not conflicting.
The new code allows to get as much 'tried' bits as
possible. It also doesn't require extra locking attempt
for that.
Patch intoduces new inodebits field try_bits which contains
optinal bits to get if possible without blocking. Those
bits are checked along with the required bits and only
non-blocked are added to the new IBITS lock.

Signed-off-by: Mikhal Pershin <mike.pershin@intel.com>
Change-Id: I78d181243556e65d3bd702d332307d0723d5b665
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/25262
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
